items.title: The multi-million-dollar tourist hotspot where locals can't afford to live

children.content: A tourist hotspot with skyrocketing rents, low rental vacancies and a dearth of public housing has found a way to help find homes for those who can't afford to live in their own region.

items.title: Spectacular Irankunda goal gives Socceroos lead against Türkiye

children.content: The Socceroos begin their 2026 World Cup journey in Vancouver with their opening Group D match against a talented Türkiye. Follow live.

image.altText: Two soccer players in green and gold celebrate a goal

items.title: 'A footballer's dream': From humble beginnings to the world stage

children.content: The Touré brothers snuck into their local football field after hours to play the game they loved, while the Yengi brothers kicked around makeshift balls made of plastic bags. Two of them have now made it onto the world stage as Socceroos.

image.altText: composite of a Mo Toure as a young boy in a soccer field and him as an adult in a Socceroos guernsey

items.title: T-Birds cap off near-flawless season with record number of wins

children.content: This year's record-breaking Adelaide Thunderbirds are shifting their focus to the Super Netball finals, with a third premiership in four years on offer.

image.altText: A Thunderbirds player and Lightning player reach across each other for the ball

items.title: Swans steal thriller with spirited second-half comeback against Port

children.content: Sydney's big guns all stand up tall in the final quarter to help secure a three-point win over Port Adelaide in Adelaide as North Melbourne escapes against West Coast.

image.altText: Isaac Heeney handballs while being tackled

items.title: From wine to water, Adelaide Hills charity brings hope to kids in Kenya

children.content: From the Adelaide Hills to refugee camps in Kenya, how one young Aussie has made it his mission to help.

image.altText: A group of young boys smile and pose for a photo in rural Kenya.

items.title: SANFL players consider industrial action as hourly salary drops to $8

children.content: The SANFL Players' Association is discussing the potential of a strike in response to a 50 per cent decline in pay over the past 20 years.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie sitting on a bench in a grassed area

items.title: 'Lambo Guy' Adrian Portelli sues former minister over lottery comments

children.content: Melbourne "rewards club" lottery billionaire Adrian Portelli has launched a defamation lawsuit against former SA minister Andrea Michaels over comments she made about a $3.5 million home prize draw.

image.altText: A man holding 100 dollar bills in his hand

items.title: Why young people say the social media ban is not working six months on

children.content: Research suggests many under-16s are still active on social media platforms. BTN High explores why and tests the accuracy of popular technology used to estimate age.

image.altText: A teenage boy holds up a mobile phone with the number 16 on it. Logos for Facebook, X and other platforms swirl near his face.

items.title: Gus Lamont's grandparent fined over gun offence, unrelated to 4yo's disappearance

children.content: The grandparent of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont has been fined $10,500 for possession of a gun silencer at the family's Oak Park Station, in South Australia's far north. Police have previously said the offence is not related to his disappearance.

image.altText: A person wearing with shoulder-length blonde hair wearing an Akubra hat and maroon jacket, holding a black handbag

items.title: Ex pro cyclist Rohan Dennis reported for driving while disqualified, ABC understands

children.content: Rohan Dennis was handed a suspended sentence last year for the events leading up to the death of his Olympian wife Melissa Hoskins in December 2023.

image.altText: A white sedan on the tray of a tow truck parked in front of a police car

items.title: Reuben Kaye reckons you can joke about anything — 'it's just how you do it'

children.content: The new artistic director of the Adelaide Cabaret Festival shares what's unique about the art form in Australia and what his teenage self would think about who he is today.

image.altText: A portrait of Reuben Kaye, 41, smiling, mouth open, one finger between his teeth, with a cheeky expression.

items.title: Port River 'stud muffin' among spate of dolphin deaths in Adelaide

children.content: Environmental authorities are investigating what caused the death of a Port River dolphin, believed to be Zoom, this week after several dolphin carcasses washed up on Adelaide beaches in recent days.

image.altText: The fin of a dolphin coming up to surface in a murky river with green mangroves on the shore.

items.title: Bushies reunite for bikes, beers and banter as record rain lifts spirits

children.content: The trip to Marree in South Australia looked a little different for competitors in this year's annual gymkhana and motorkhana, with the outback blooming after record-breaking rain.

items.title: Feral sambar deer likely brought to SA for hunting purposes

children.content: One of Australia's largest feral deer species was spotted in SA's Riverland for the first time earlier this month. While its existence is being investigated, the Invasive Species Council says it was likely transported across state lines for hunting purposes.

image.altText: Fallow deer seen through a rifle scope.

items.title: 'Silly thing': Man fined thousands for dumping tyres in heritage-listed area

children.content: A young man who could have paid $115 to discard a trailer load of tyres has been fined thousands for illegally dumping them down a mine shaft in a national heritage-listed area — and ordered to clean it up.

image.altText: A pile of old tyres sitting on an empty lot.

items.title: Line dancing resurgence draws gen Z out to socialise in Adelaide

children.content: It's seen by many as a way to socialise and meet new people in an era where such activities are at a low point due to the ongoing impacts of COVID-19, screen time and social media.

image.altText: A large group of people dance on a dance floor at a pub

items.title: Fraudsters jailed for stealing millions from 'older and vulnerable' victims

children.content: Two men who "exploited the vulnerability" of multiple victims by swindling millions of dollars in a fraudulent property investment scheme have been jailed for the "undoubtedly serious" offending.

image.altText: A composite image of a bald man and a man with a beard

items.title: How this globe shows the incredibly diverse story of our Socceroos

children.content: The Socceroos squad is like no other that's ever been sent to represent us at a World Cup. See the extraordinary journeys that forged a team to represent modern Australia on the world stage.

items.title: 'Tragic situation': Perth girl shot dead by police overseas

children.content: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.
